name: batch_read
description: Read multiple files in one call with clear separators between them. Supports line numbers, head/tail limiting per file. More compact than chaining separate cat commands.
when_to_use: Use when you need to inspect multiple source files at once (e.g., reading related modules, test files, or config files). Replaces 2-5+ separate cat commands with a single call. The --head and --tail flags prevent token overflow by limiting output per file. The --lines flag adds line numbers for reference.

parameters.name: files
parameters.description: One or more file paths to read. Can include glob patterns (e.g., src/*.ts).

parameters.name: --lines/-n
parameters.description: Show line numbers for each file.

parameters.name: --head/-h
parameters.description: Show only the first N lines of each file (useful for large files).

parameters.name: --tail/-t
parameters.description: Show only the last N lines of each file.

examples.expected: Content of both files separated by '===== filename =====' headers.
First 30 lines with line numbers of each .ts file in src/.
Last 20 lines of both package.json and README.md.

cost_saving_rationale: Replaces 2-5 separate cat commands (or cat && echo && cat chains) with a single script call. Each separate cat command is a separate tool call that adds overhead. The batch approach reduces token waste from repeating similar commands and the shell escaping needed to chain them.
